_doc: Single source of truth for paper-trader fees, slippage, gates, and risk rules. Both the TypeScript live trader (artifacts/api-server/src/lib/trading-constants.ts) and the Python backtester (artifacts/ml-engine/app/backtest/contract.py) import this file. Drift between the two is a correctness bug.

label_thresholds_percent._doc: Training-only label band. Strictly LESS THAN outcome_thresholds_percent so the model learns directional structure on the bulk of moves; adjudication still uses outcome_thresholds_percent so a 'correct' UP must still clear the round-trip cost. Tuned 2026-04-21 (task #95) so the up/down classes carry meaningful expected-return mass — previously 88% of bars were labeled 'stable' and the model never learned direction.

label_thresholds_percent_per_coin._doc: Per-coin overrides on top of label_thresholds_percent. Each override MUST stay strictly less than outcome_thresholds_percent for the same timeframe (test_per_coin_label_thresholds_below_outcome_thresholds enforces this) so a model trade still has to clear the round-trip cost to count as 'correct'. Overrides may be tighter OR wider than the timeframe default depending on the coin's realized vol. History: tuned 2026-04-22 (task #120) to a single TIGHTER 0.04% band so 10 quiet coins crossed the bar on enough holdout rows to learn direction. Re-tuned 2026-04-23 (task #318) for the 5 coins closest to clearing the verification gate: realized 5m vol on the training window was ~0.85-0.97% MAD per coin, so the 0.04% band was orders of magnitude below typical move size — the bulk of training rows were UP/DOWN with the modal class barely above 0.50, leaving the multinomial-logistic baseline parked near coin-flip and the booster with no consolidation to lift. The new per-coin 5m thresholds are derived as `0.25 × MAD(forward 5m return, training 80%)` clipped to [0.10%, 0.30%] — wide enough to consolidate the modal class share above 0.50 on the holdout while still leaving 30-40%% of training rows in the directional buckets. The full methodology / outcome are in artifacts/ml-engine/reports/20260423T010000Z-task318-per-coin-thresholds.md. Values are coin_id -> {timeframe -> percent}.

quant_brain._doc: Phase 5 cutover. enabled is the persisted-default for the brain feature flag (the runtime flag in app_settings.quant_brain_enabled overrides this; QUANT_BRAIN_FORCE_OFF=1 env var beats both). auto_revert defines the configurable safety guard that disables the quant brain when the quant model drifts low vs the offline backtest baseline on the majority of evaluable coins for a sustained window. With the brain disabled, the live model-authored path abstains; there is no LLM fallback.

decision_thresholds._doc: Live quant-brain emission gate (artifacts/api-server/src/lib/quant-brain.ts). The Python backtester (artifacts/ml-engine/app/backtest/simulator.py) reads the SAME values so offline reports are a faithful prediction of live behaviour. min_expected_return_pct_factor multiplies round_trip_cost_pct*100 to derive the |expRet| floor — this keeps the brain in lock-step with the paper-trader EV gate (gates_baseline.MIN_EV_VS_COST.value × ROUND_TRIP_COST_PCT × 100 by construction). policy_version is stamped on backtest_report.json so a stale report against an old policy is loud. Calibrated 2026-04-22 (task #130): on the 5m walk-forward OOS slice (880 rows) the regression head's |expRet| distribution caps at ~0.19% (median 0.033%, p95 0.108%), while the previous factor=3.0 (=0.9% required) blocked 100% of signals — so the backtester emitted 0 trades regardless of any other tuning and the deploy verdict was vacuous. The new factor=0.5 (=0.15% required, half of the 0.30% round-trip cost) emits 12 directional signals on the same slice and lets the simulator actually exercise the downstream MIN_CONFIDENCE_TO_TRADE / fee_gate / counter_trend gates so the report reflects what the live system would have done. min_directional_edge raised 0.02→0.05: median observed |p_up-p_down| is 0.17, so 0.05 keeps coin-flip edges out without touching the meaningful mass. min_directional_prob held at 0.08 (median dir_prob=0.36, well above). The study does NOT call this a deploy candidate — only 2 trades survived all live gates and both were losses; the verdict remains do-not-deploy until the model produces real edge.

trailing_stop._doc: expiry_extension_ms MUST match the inline trailingExtensionMs map in artifacts/api-server/src/lib/paper-trader.ts. Drift is a parity bug.

regime_classifier._doc: Price-only subset of artifacts/api-server/src/lib/regime-detector.ts:classifyRegime. Backtester does not have historical fearGreed / btcDominance feeds, so those signals are dropped — both classifiers must agree on the price-only branch.

shadow_cutover_gate._doc: Phase 4 → Phase 5 promotion rule. The Quant Shadow panel surfaces a 'ready to evaluate cutover' badge once these are all met on resolved shadow predictions per timeframe.

backtest_deploy_gate._doc: If backtest results clear all of these, the model is approved for shadow-mode (Phase 4). Failing any line means: do NOT promote.

champion_challenger._doc: Phase 5 — model registry lifecycle gates. A challenger may be PROMOTED to champion only when ALL of these hold over its shadow window. Rollback is unconditional. min_shadow_samples is the floor on resolved shadow predictions; min_net_edge_lift_vs_champion is the percentage-point lift in net edge (after fees) the challenger must show over the current champion; max_drawdown_pct caps realized worst-window equity drawdown on the same shadow stream; min_passing_regimes_share requires the challenger to clear net-positive net edge in at least this fraction of the regimes it has samples in (so a model that only works in one regime cannot be promoted on aggregate average alone).

portfolio_constraints._doc: Phase 5 — fleet-level constraints applied by the unified decision engine BEFORE a position opens. Mirrors the per-position gates in `risk` but acts across the whole open book. max_correlated_exposure_pct caps total notional in coins from the same sector. max_sector_exposure_pct caps notional per sector. max_beta_to_btc bounds the equally-weighted beta (vs BTC) of the open book; new positions that would push the book above this limit are skipped. regime_budget_pct caps the share of total notional that can be open in any single regime label, so a single regime call cannot dominate the portfolio. enabled is a kill switch — when false, all portfolio gates pass through.
